questions.question: What is the Law of Conservation of Mass?
questions.correct_answer: Mass can neither be created nor destroyed in a chemical reaction
questions.explanation: The total mass of the products in a chemical reaction is equal to the total mass of the reactants.

questions.question: What is the Law of Constant Proportions?
questions.correct_answer: In a chemical substance, elements are always present in definite proportions by mass
questions.explanation: This is also known as the law of definite proportions; for example, the ratio of H:O in water is always 1:8.

questions.question: Define an atom.
questions.correct_answer: The smallest unit of an element that maintains its chemical identity
questions.explanation: Atoms are the building blocks of all matter and are extremely small (measured in nanometers).

questions.question: What is an atomic mass unit (u)?
questions.correct_answer: A mass unit equal to exactly 1/12th the mass of one atom of carbon-12
questions.explanation: It is the standard unit used to express the relative masses of atoms and molecules.

questions.question: What is a molecule?
questions.correct_answer: A group of two or more atoms that are chemically bonded together
questions.explanation: It is the smallest particle of an element or compound capable of independent existence.

questions.question: What is meant by 'atomicity'?
questions.correct_answer: The number of atoms constituting a molecule
questions.explanation: For example, Helium is monoatomic (1), Oxygen is diatomic (2), and Phosphorus is tetra-atomic (4).

questions.question: What is an ion?
questions.correct_answer: A charged particle formed by the loss or gain of electrons
questions.explanation: Positively charged ions are cations; negatively charged ions are anions.

questions.question: What is a polyatomic ion?
questions.correct_answer: A group of atoms carrying a fixed net charge
questions.explanation: Examples include the Nitrate ion ($NO_3^-$) and Ammonium ion ($NH_4^+$).

questions.question: What is valency?
questions.correct_answer: The combining capacity of an element
questions.explanation: It determines how atoms of one element will combine with atoms of another to form a compound.

questions.question: What is molecular mass?
questions.correct_answer: The sum of the atomic masses of all the atoms in a molecule
questions.explanation: It is expressed in atomic mass units (u).

notes.title: Chemical Combination Laws
notes.content: Established by Lavoisier and Proust, these laws formed the foundation of chemical science.

notes.title: Dalton's Atomic Theory
notes.content: Proposed that all matter is made of indivisible atoms and explained the laws of chemical combination.

notes.title: Writing Formulae
notes.content: Uses the criss-cross method where valencies of constituent elements are crossed to become subscripts.

facts.fact: The term 'atom' was coined by Democritus, meaning 'indivisible'.
Noble gases like Argon and Helium exist as single atoms (monoatomic).
